_doc: Editor steering notes for the conference preview agent. Authoritative — these override prior knowledge and any conflicting context-data interpretation. Keep each note focused — 1–3 short sentences for stable programs, 4–8 sentences when the team has multiple narrative threads or league volatility requires more calibration nuance. Empty strings are dropped silently. Loaded by build_conference_context.py from /cfb-research/writer_notes/<season>/<conf>.json.
_usage: conference_note: 1–3 sentences with two purposes — (a) the meta-narrative for the year (e.g., 'The realignment dust has settled but the talent gap between the top three and the rest is still widening.'), and (b) calibration the agent needs to right-size confidence: what 'good' looks like in this league. SEC example: 'In modern SEC: 7-1 in conf typically reaches the title game; 9-0 has never happened. 9 overall wins is a historic season.' MAC example: 'In the MAC, 8 conference wins is championship caliber; 6-2 contends.' Combine narrative + calibration when both matter.

team_notes.<slug>: 1–8 sentences about that specific team — what the data won't tell the agent (a coaching change, a critical transfer, an injury, an off-field story, the angle the writeup should take). Longer notes are appropriate when a team has multiple narrative threads or the league requires more calibration nuance.
Slugs match the canonical team slugs in build_team_context.py / scrape_team_context.py.
conference_note: The ACC is a P4 conference but trails the Big Ten and SEC in talent and funding, with the bottom of the league usually housing the worst P4 teams in the country. Three storylines define the 2026 ACC: (1) Coaching turbulence is the league's defining theme — Belichick-Lombardi enter year 2 of a flopping experiment at UNC, James Franklin lands at Virginia Tech with credibility his last stop didn't end on, Norvell survives at FSU only because the buyout was prohibitive, Dabo's late era continues to slide at Clemson, and longtime mainstays Doeren and Narduzzi are running out of NIL-era runway; this is a transitional year for the league's coaching map. (2) Duke's encore as defending champion is the year's biggest individual-team question — Manny Diaz won the league with a 7-5 overall record but lost starting QB Darian Mensah and top WR Cooper Barkate to Miami via the portal on the final day possible; whether Duke regresses hard or Diaz reloads will define both the title race and the conference's narrative. (3) Miami's title-game runner-up encore — they made the national championship game in 2025 and lost to Indiana; the question is whether they can repeat the run or if last year was a peak that's hard to maintain. Conference-record calibration: the ACC plays an unbalanced schedule — 12 teams play 9 conference games while 5 teams play only 8, so always lead with the conference number (not overall) and note the denominator when relevant. The ACC title game qualifier is conference record only, not overall — Duke proved the structural quirk in 2025 with a 6-2 conference record winning the league despite a 7-5 overall regular season (one non-conference win all year). A 7-2 conference team is a title-game contender even if they're 8-4 overall. Volatility calibration: the ACC is the most parity-driven of the P4 leagues. Outside of a small group of stable programs at the top, the rest of the league churns through tiers year-to-year, with QB health and two coin-flip games typically separating an 8-win season from a 4-win season. When team_notes give a 3-tier range (e.g., Tier 2-4), that's not a hedge — it's the league norm. The agent should NOT over-anchor on a single 'centering' tier; instead treat the range itself as the projection. A team at Tier 2-4 means 'either it works and they're contending for the title game or it doesn't and they're fighting for a bowl' — both branches are real. ACC tier framework (tiers describe team caliber, NOT exact record): Tier 1 = ACC title-game favorite if healthy and CFP-bound (typically as the conference auto-bid, not an at-large); Tier 2 = title-game contender, dark-horse CFP at-large; Tier 3 = middle tier and title-game outsider, in the contention mix but outside CFP; Tier 4 = rebuilding year, fighting to get to a bowl game; Tier 5 = down year, where reaching bowl eligibility would itself be the ceiling. When team_notes give a range, the agent should treat the wider boundary as the realistic downside — but per the volatility note above, 3-tier ranges are common here and the range itself should be framed as the projection rather than collapsing to a single tier.

team_notes.boston-college: Tier 4-5 ACC team. Probably the least promising ACC team, BC has little resources or seemingly buy in to compete in this new NIL/transfer portal era right now.  HC Bill O'Brien has had a good career that feels like it's ending with a thud, going 9-16 in his two seasons at BC.  They have a three way battle for the starting QB and need WRs to emerge such as Johnathan Montague Jr. who they expect big things from after missing 2025 with an ACL tear.  They added RB Evan Dickens from Liberty via the portal who rushed for over 1300 yards last year, but the OL will be made up from 5 starters from the transfer portal who are all either G6 or FCS adds. Defensive front 7 looks average at best although they have some good promising CBs and a decent duo at Safety.
team_notes.california: Tier 3-4 ACC team. Cal has famously lacked backing by the administration to compete at football in recent years, but that may be changing after hiring HC Tosh Lupoi who has brought a new enthusiasm to the program.  QB sensation Jaron-Keawe Sagapolutele was a freshman revelation and a key resign by Lupoi who made JKS his top priority to retain. They added WRs Chase Hendricks from Ohio and Ian Strong from Rutgers to give JKS some weapons to throw to, as well as TE Dorian Thomas from New Mexico. The OL is a mesh of portal adds who will need time to gel.  The defense is Lupoi's specialty and he'll need it to build one out of 16 portal adds along with only 3 returning starters.
team_notes.clemson: Tier 2-4 ACC team.  Clemson was at the top of the ACC but HC Dabo Swinney was reluctant to adapt to transfer portal era and it has cost them.  Clemson had 9 players drafted from a team that only went 7-6 last season, a huge disappointment after being a projected top 5 team.  HC Swinney is ACCs longest tenured head coach and is the greatest coach in Clemson history, but his era has been declining for years.  Big rebuild year after graduation losses.  QB Christopher Vizzina started one game last year, limited experience.  WR duo of T.J. Moore and Bryant Wesco Jr. is a top ACC unit, RB has been a big issue for Clemson in recent years. Defense rebuilding, DE Will Heldt one of best in CFB.
team_notes.duke: Tier 2-4 ACC team.  Duke exposed the mediocrity of the ACC as a conference by winning the ACC Championship despite a 7-5 regular season record by going 6-2 in the conference.  HC Manny Diaz is a really good one that gets a lot out of his teams just like 2025 proved.  They lost starting QB Darian Mensah via transfer portal on final day possible to Miami along with WR Cooper Barkate.  They signed QB Walker Eget from San Jose State but he is recovering from an ACL that kept him out of spring.  RB Nate Sheppard had a great freshman season and TE Jeremiah Hasley is a true weapon and a PFF top 10 TE.  The defense in 2025 was bad so Duke signed 5 DB portal adds to help boost the secondary.
team_notes.florida-state: Tier 3-4 ACC team.  HC Mike Norvell would have been fired after last year had FSU been able to afford his buyout.  Norvell has gone 7-17 the past two years and this year does not look much better.  They signed QB Ashton Daniels from Auburn, an average at best QB with good mobility.  They lost a lot of key players to the transfer portal, although they did sign a lot of experienced players adding 147 starts via portal adds.  Their Offensive coordinator, Gus Malzahn, retired in February and Norvell will call plays himself this fall.  They have a great WR in Duce Robinson and have some decent RBs like Ousmane Kromah who was decent as a freshman last year.  The only experience on the OL is 5 starters from the transfer portal, OL has been a disaster the past two seasons. The defense has some good players but the program vibes are so low it's difficult to see enough wins to save Norvell's job after this year.
team_notes.georgia-tech: Tier 2-4 ACC team. HC Brent Key has been a good hire now entering his fifth season with a 27-20 record.  This will be a rebuilding year after losing all everything QB Haynes King and OC Buster Faulkner who left for the Florida Gators, taking a few players with him.  They signed QB Alberto Mendoza, brother of Heisman QB Fernando Mendoza, who he backed up at Indiana last year.  RB Malachi Hosley is back and Justice Haynes was added from Michigan to form a good duo. WR is a big question mark but TE Gavin Harris who they signed from New Mexico State is a good one. The OL is the big question replacing three starters.  Reports are their defensive front 7 is the most confident unit which is a surprise from a 2025 defense that ranked #95 nationally in PPA.
team_notes.louisville: Tier 2-3 ACC team.  HC Jeff Brohm is a great one, an Offensive mastermind who has won at least 9 games every season at Louisville.  Brohm coached teams are famous for pulling one big upset a season. Brother Brian Brohm is the OC and a good one. QB Lincoln Kienholz lost the QB battle at Ohio State to Julian Sayin in 2025, has little game experience but Jeff Brohm is a QB mastermind and trusts him.  RBs Isaac Brown and Keyjuan Brown a good duo, WR rebuilt via the transfer portal.  DE Clev Lubin one of CFBs best, DE AJ Green also good, LBs very strong lead by Antonio Watts.  OL rebuilt via the portal with 3 starters added from other schools.
team_notes.miami: Tier 1 ACC team. CFP team in 2025, won 3 games to make the national title game but lost to Indiana.  Has yet to win the ACC championship since joining the conference in 2004.  Took QB Darian Mensah from Duke via transfer portal on final day possible, WR Cooper Barkate as well, which caused some controversy.  HC Mario Cristobal has built Miami by emphasizing strong line play with good OL & DL, although both must rebuild this year. WR Malachi Toney was a freshman sensation last year, great weapon. Defense was dominant last year, will be good again this year but replacing important leaders.
team_notes.nc-state: Tier 2-4 ACC team.  HC Dave Doeren is second most tenured ACC coach, has been good but not great with a definite ceiling. QB CJ Bailey is dynamic but must limit turnovers, RB Jayden Scott had an impressive freshman debut last year.  WR built by portal additions such as Victor Snow from Buffalo. OL looks like a strong unit with experience.  Defense has regressed some in recent years and is rebuilding this year, questions about pass rush. Are fans/administration tiring of HC Doeren?  He's been mentioned as potentially being on the hot seat, last year started 3-0, ended 3-0 but went 2-5 in between. Went 1-4 on the road last year.
team_notes.north-carolina: Tier 3-5 ACC team.  HC Bill Belichick flopped in his first season last year after arriving at North Carolina with all the fanfare and expectations that come with a eight time NFL Super Bowl champion head coach.  GM Michael Lombardi has been a joke and a bad hire, famously referring that North Carolina would be 33rd NFL team.  The Tar Heels went 4-8 and finished 2-7 with a particularly bad offense.  They hired famous OC Bobby Petrino to fix that offense, although the QBs they added like Billy Edwards Jr from Wisconsin who has been average at best, and Taron Dickens from FCS Western Carolina who put up huge numbers but is an unknown and doesn't join the team until spring.  Their hope is the defense can improve and hold enough opponents down until the offense finds any rhythm, but I'm highly skeptical this Belichick/Lombardi experiment will work in CFB.
team_notes.pittsburgh: Tier 2-3 ACC team.  HC Pat Narduzzi is another ACC mainstay in his 12th year, although his tenure has taken a step back in the NIL/portal era due to limited resources. Pitt has the 2nd lowest ranked portal class in the P4, although they brought in a good 2026 recruiting class.  QB Mason Heintschel was a freshman revelation last year and is a good building block along with sophmore RB Ja'Kyrian Turner.  The OL looks like a real strength with 6 players with full time starting experience that includes 2 portal additions.  Secondary should be good with CB duo of Shadarian Harrison and Shawn Lee Jr. plus Safety Cruce Brookins.  Panthers have potential this year if WRs emerge and a pass rush can develop.
team_notes.smu: Tier 2-3 ACC team.  Joined ACC prior to 2024 season and made ACC title game and College Football playoffs, lost in first round to Penn State.  HC Rhett Lashlee is a good one that has won 31 games in his last 3 seasons.  QB Kevin Jennings is electric but must cut down turnovers, have good RB/WR with some portal additions.  OL looks strong with 3 very good returning starting with All American candidate OT PJ Williams. DL rebuilt by portal additions, LBs strength of defense.
team_notes.stanford: Tier 4-5 ACC team.  Very difficult job with more transfer portal limitations than any other P4 program.  GM Andrew Luck is former All American QB, hired another former Stanford QB in Tavita Pritchard as head coach.  That may have been because it was the best candidate that would actually take the job.  QB is Davis Warren, a transfer from Michigan with 9 career starts and real limitations.  RB Micah Ford is a bright spot after a good freshman debut last year, but WR, OL and honestly most of the defense are questions without real ACC level answers, at least on paper. LB Matt Rose and Safety Jay Green are good players.
team_notes.syracuse: Tier 3-5 ACC team. HC Fran Brown made a big splash in his first season in 2024 taking Syracuse to 10 wins, but then plummeted to 3-9 last year after QB Steve Angeli went down with a torn ACL injury.  Hired new DC Vince Kehres from Toledo who is a big upgrade for the staff, hopefully he can fix what has been a poor Syracuse defense.  That defense should improve with LBs Chris D'Appolonia and Antoine Deslauriers (from Toledo with Kehres) good building blocks and a good duo at CB in Demetres Samuel Jr. and Nazir Ward (from WKU). WR added Cole Weaver from Miami-OH but freshman Calvin Russell tore his ACL in spring and is out until at least November leaving questions about depth.  Added Ahmad Miller at RB from Jacksonville State who's a good one, the offensive line being a central question about the offense.
team_notes.virginia: Tier 2-4 ACC team.  Virginia had a great season last year making the ACC title game and saving HC Tony Elliott's job and earning him an extension through 2030.  They have committed resources to the transfer portal and have focused on signing older, experienced players which paid off big time last year. Starting QB will likely be Beau Pribula, a transfer from Missouri who is a mobile threat but an ankle injury ended his 2025 season.  RB room is good led by 3 transfer portal additions Jekail Middlebrook, Peyton Lewis, and Solomon Beebe. OL has three starters back. Defense looks solid with good experience, including 4 DL and 7 DB starters from portal additions.
team_notes.virginia-tech: Tier 2-4 ACC team.  Hired former Penn State coach James Franklin who brings a new credibility to a VT program that has been down for many years.  Former HC Brent Pry was fired from VT last year but remained popular, and was hired as the defensive coordinator by Franklin.  New OC Ty Howle was at Penn State with Franklin last year, but he's likely a first time play caller.  QB Ethan Grunkemeyer was also with Franklin at Penn State last year, played decent in a difficult spot after starter was injured. RB Marcellous Hawkins returns after a decent year in 2025, Bill Davis from Louisiana University added as well.  TE Luke Reynolds, also from Penn State, looks to be a breakout star for VT this year. OL has four experienced starters but still a question, defense looks solid but has been inconsistent at best in recent years.
team_notes.wake-forest: Tier 2-4 ACC team.  HC Jake Dickert was hired prior to last years 2025 season from Washington State and took a Wake Forest team projected to finish at the bottom of the ACC to a 9-4 first season, beating Mississippi State in the Duke's Mayo Bowl. Wake Forest is a program that has less resources than most. New QB Gio Lopez was added from North Carolina after a poor season in 2025, but he was a good starter in 2024 at South Alabama. He had a good spring and looks to bounce back at Wake Forest.  They added RB/WR via the portal, a mix G6 starters and P4 players stuck on depth charts elsewhere.  The OL has 4 experienced starters, although three are from the FCS level and have been labeled as physically behind ACC standards.  Defensive front 7 looks solid with experience led by DE Langston Hardy who led ACC with 17.0 TFLs in 2025. Along with the OL, CB looks like a question going into the fall.
